title: tasks-mux gaps: priorities, dependencies, search, status history, bulk ops
summary: Implement the narrowed task-management primitives needed before tasks-mux can serve as the unified routing hub: priority, dependencies, search/filtering, status history, bulk operations, state-machine validation, and audit logging.
acceptanceCriteria: Breakpoints expose canonical priority: low | medium | high | critical with compatibility behavior for existing context.urgency data.
Breakpoints expose dependsOn: string[] and dependency blocking prevents resolution or approval until prerequisites are complete.
The backend contract exposes searchBreakpoints(query) with filters for status, assignee/responder, priority, free text, projectId, and repoId.
Breakpoint state transitions append status history/timeline entries with timestamps.
Bulk approve, bulk close, and bulk reassign operations provide per-breakpoint success/failure results and preserve partial failure evidence.
Invalid status transitions are rejected consistently by direct operations and bulk operations.
Audit log entries record who changed what and when, including before/after values where available.
git-native, server, GitHub Issues, client, CLI, and MCP surfaces either support the new contract or fail with explicit backend capability errors covered by tests.
Existing breakpoint files, proven-answer behavior, ask/answer/status/poll/claim commands, and responder routing metadata remain backward-compatible.
nonGoals: Do not implement the broader #596 items unless needed as extension hooks: notifications, SLA metrics, discussion threads, escalation chains, interactive forms, data export, or backup.
Do not wire SDK or agent-platform routing through tasks-mux in this issue; #634 only makes tasks-mux ready for that routing work.
Do not replace git-native persistence with a database.
Do not add shell-oriented subtasks to the process definition.

plannedPhases.name: Issue context
plannedPhases.purpose: Read #634, duplicate parent #596, comments, labels, related issues, and routing-gap design docs.

plannedPhases.name: Reuse audit
plannedPhases.purpose: Render the required reuse-audit findings and map existing tasks-mux seams before introducing new contract surface.

plannedPhases.name: Architecture plan
plannedPhases.purpose: Design additive schema, state machine, dependency blocking, audit/history model, backend API, CLI/MCP/client surfaces, and migration rules.

plannedPhases.name: Regression plan
plannedPhases.purpose: Write red-first acceptance and compatibility tests across schema, backends, client, CLI, MCP, docs, and legacy fixtures.

plannedPhases.name: Implementation slices
plannedPhases.purpose: Implement shared contract, git-native behavior, server/GitHub/client parity, and CLI/MCP/docs in gated slices.

plannedPhases.name: Verification and review
plannedPhases.purpose: Run targeted and full verification, then perform compatibility and routing-hub readiness review.

plannedPhases.name: Final acceptance
plannedPhases.purpose: Trace every issue deliverable to implementation, tests, docs, and verification evidence.

breakpoints.when: Issue #634's duplicate/closed state conflicts with the requested implementation scope.
options: Switch implementation run to duplicate parent issue

breakpoints.when: Final acceptance finds an unresolved compatibility or status-model decision.

qualityGates: Reuse audit begins with the required REVIEW BEFORE PROCEEDING marker and lists reusable/missing tasks-mux seams.
Red tests fail for expected missing-feature reasons before production implementation.
Priority and dependsOn are canonical schema fields and legacy breakpoints still parse.
Dependency blocking is enforced across answer/approve/complete and bulk approval paths.
searchBreakpoints supports combined status, assignee/responder, priority, and text filters or returns explicit unsupported capability errors.
Bulk operations return per-id results, record audit entries, and reject invalid transitions without corrupting unrelated breakpoints.
Status history records every transition with timestamps and actor/source metadata when available.
CLI and MCP tools expose search and bulk operations with tests.
Server and GitHub backends preserve hidden payload compatibility and mapping parity where supported.
Final review score is at least 90 with no blocking findings.
